1.1.0025.answer: Summary of Insulated Rubber Glove Periodic Inspection Procedures   This document outlines the procedures for the periodic inspection of insulated rubber gloves. Key aspects include:   1. Inspection Cycle and Frequency: Gloves must undergo periodic inspection at least once every six months.   2. Inspection Records:   Inspection Chart: A standardized form ("Form 1 Periodic Inspection Chart for Electrical Insulated Rubber Gloves for High Voltage/Low Voltage") is used to record inspection data for each glove. This chart is managed collectively by section. Chart Contents: The chart includes the inspection date (as per the manufacturer's report), reviewer, investigators, approver, glove control number, glove specifications (name, model number, manufacturer), location of use, manufacturer's inspection results (external inspection and pressure resistance test – "○" for both good, "×" if either is defective), and any additional notes (e.g., new purchase, disposal, replacement).  3. Glove Control Number:   Format: A five-character alphanumeric code (Section Abbreviation - Glove Serial Number). Section Abbreviation: Unique alphanumeric code assigned per section. Glove Serial Number: Numeric sequence (001-999). Marking: The control number is clearly marked (approximately 10mm square, black permanent marker) on the back of the wrist of each glove.  4. Inspection Pass Indication: Each glove is stamped with the date of passing the inspection. This date determines the next inspection date.   5. Manufacturer's Inspection Report:   Content: Includes external inspection results and pressure resistance test results (both must be "good"). Additionally, it must legally include the manufacturer's name, inspection date, method, location, inspector's name, and any remedial actions taken. High Voltage Test Values: 20,000V (new), 10,000V (periodic). Low Voltage Test Values: 3,000V (new), 1,500V (periodic). Defective Gloves: Gloves marked as "defective" must be discarded and replaced with spares. Inspection Provider: Kyowa PV Co., Ltd. (via WWW).  6. Record Retention: Inspection charts and reports must be retained as quality records for a minimum of three years, as required by law.

file:For_the_safe_operation_of_forklifts.answer: The following points apply to forklift driver qualifications.  In order to operate a forklift with a maximum load of 1 ton or more, a person who has completed the skill training course for forklift operation (Article 20, Item 11 of the Enforcement Order of the Industrial Safety and Health Act) or other qualified person is required. In addition, the applicant must operate a forklift with a maximum load of less than 1 ton and must have completed a special training course for forklift operation (Item 5 of Article 36 of the Ordinance on Industrial Safety and Health (Hereinafter referred to as "Anei-no-kori".)). Also, the above qualifications alone do not allow drivers to drive on public roads.

1.1.0040.answer: The following points should be noted when carrying out cargo handling work using a forklift.  ・To determine the speed limit for cargo handling workshops (Article 151 paragraph 5 of the Safety and Health Ordinance). ・In principle, workers shall not be allowed to enter places where workers are liable to be exposed to dangers due to contact with cargo. When it is necessary to let a person enter, a guide shall be assigned (Article 151 7 of the Safety and Health Ordinance). When placing a guide, set a certain signal (Article 151 8 of the Safety and Health Ordinance). ・In principle, workers shall not be allowed to enter under the load of forks of a forklift. When it is necessary to allow a person to enter, to have the person use safety supports, safety blocks, etc. (Article 151 ‐ 9 of the Safety and Health Ordinance).  ・To prevent a forklift from being used outside its intended purpose (lifting, lowering, etc.) (Article 151 14 of the Safety and Health Ordinance).

1.1.0039.answer: The following are the conditions to be observed by the operator when carrying out cargo handling work using a forklift.  ・Observe the speed limit in the cargo handling workshop (Article 151 paragraph 5 of the Safety and Health Ordinance). ・When a guide is assigned, follow the guidance of the guide (Article 151 ‐ 7 of the Safety and Health Ordinance and Article 151 ‐ 8 of the Safety and Health Ordinance). ・In principle, do not enter under the forks of a forklift. When instructed to enter, be sure to use safety supports, safety blocks, etc. (Article 151 ‐ 9 of the Safety and Health Ordinance). ・When a forklift is stopped, to surely take runaway prevention measures (Article 151 11 of the Safety and Health Ordinance). ・Not to put a worker on a place other than the riding seat of a forklift (Article 151 13 of the Safety and Health Ordinance). ・Off-purpose use on forklifts (elevation of △, etc.) or not (Article 151 14 of the Safety and Health Ordinance). ・Not to use a forklift exceeding the allowable load or other capacity (Article 151 20 of the Safety and Health Ordinance). ・Wear a seat belt when driving (if one is available). ・To prevent accidents caused by being caught between the mast and the headguard, do not lean out of the driver's seat. ・When using a forklift in which the driver's seat is raised and lowered, take crash prevention measures such as the use of safety belts. Do not make a sudden stop or turn. ・When driving back, make sure to check the rear (direction of travel). ・When moving forward with a load on a fork, check the front (blind spot of the load) thoroughly. ・When passing through the premises, walk in the safe passage and do not jump out from behind a load.

1.1.0024.answer: In order to detect damage, rubber deterioration, dirt, etc., please carry out a starting inspection by visual inspection, etc., and if there is any defect, please replace it. - Contents of the start check - Visual check: No pinholes, cuts, tears, etc. - Tensile check: There is no deterioration of rubber such as partial tension, cracks or scratches.  - Air test check: To detect pinholes, cracks, etc., conduct an air test and ensure that no air leaks. (Air test: Roll the glove from the cuff and hold it by the wrist, and press the inflated part to confirm that there is no air leakage. - Check for dirt: Make sure that the product is clean. If it is dirty, wash it with a neutral detergent. Also, dry the product before use.

Raise the Lifter: Raise the lifter using the lift lever.    Soldering Condition Verification: Check wave height, width, and solder level to ensure they meet specifications.    Resume Soldering Operation: Once soldering conditions are confirmed to be within the prescribed range and soldering performance is satisfactory, resume the soldering operation.

1.1.0036.answer: Forklift operations include the following. ・In the workshop, a walking path is defined and a line is drawn. ・During the work, only persons concerned are prohibited from entering. ・Never place a person on the claw or counterweight of a forklift. ・Forklifts have poor visibility ahead of them, so they should pay sufficient attention to the front, back, left, and right, and at the same time, when the load is large, they should carry out the following. ・driving back (Installation of back buzzer and use of fog lamp) ・Guidance by a guide shall be carried out as necessary. ・Speed limits shall be established, indicated and observed. ・Cargoes placed on pallets should be loaded safely and securely. If there is a risk of cargo collapsing, safe measures should be taken, such as wrapping the cargoes. ・When driving on the road, use a pallet, skid, etc., or mark the tip of a fork. ・Observe the four principles of stopping forklifts. 1.1.0037.answer: The following points should be considered in terms of management to prevent industrial accidents caused by forklifts.  ①Preparation and dissemination of work plans (Article of the Safety and Health Ordinance) The following are necessary when working with a forklift. ・To establish a work plan suitable for the size and topography of the work place, the type and capacity of the machine, and the type and shape of the load. ・The operation route and work method shall be indicated. ・To carry out work in accordance with a prescribed work plan. ②Appointment of a work leader (Article of the Safety and Health Ordinance) When carrying out work using a forklift, a work leader shall be appointed and cargo handling work shall be carried out based on this work plan. ③Restrictions on Employment (Article of the Safety and Health Act and item (i) of Article 0 of the Order for Enforcement of the same) Only a person who has completed the skill training course for forklift operation shall be allowed to operate a forklift (in the case of a forklift with a maximum load of more than a ton). ④Implementation of workplace patrols (Article of the Safety and Health Ordinance) It is necessary for the safety manager to periodically inspect workplaces where forklifts are being carried out to check for unsafe actions by workers. It is desirable to conduct workplace patrols even at workplaces where no safety manager has been appointed.

qf:English_Translation_FQ510-042_Wearing.answer: In order to detect damage, rubber deterioration, dirt, etc., please carry out a starting inspection by visual inspection, etc., and if there is any defect, please replace it. - Contents of the start check - Visual check: No pinholes, cuts, tears, etc. - Tensile check: There is no deterioration of rubber such as partial tension, cracks or scratches.  - Air test check: To detect pinholes, cracks, etc., conduct an air test and ensure that no air leaks. (Air test: Roll the glove from the cuff and hold it by the wrist, and press the inflated part to confirm that there is no air leakage. - Check for dirt: Make sure that the product is clean. If it is dirty, wash it with a neutral detergent. Also, dry the product before use.

Summary of Insulated Rubber Glove Periodic Inspection Procedures   This document outlines the procedures for the periodic inspection of insulated rubber gloves. Key aspects include:   1. Inspection Cycle and Frequency: Gloves must undergo periodic inspection at least once every six months.   2. Inspection Records:   Inspection Chart: A standardized form ("Form 1 Periodic Inspection Chart for Electrical Insulated Rubber Gloves for High Voltage/Low Voltage") is used to record inspection data for each glove. This chart is managed collectively by section. Chart Contents: The chart includes the inspection date (as per the manufacturer's report), reviewer, investigators, approver, glove control number, glove specifications (name, model number, manufacturer), location of use, manufacturer's inspection results (external inspection and pressure resistance test – "○" for both good, "×" if either is defective), and any additional notes (e.g., new purchase, disposal, replacement).  3. Glove Control Number:   Format: A five-character alphanumeric code (Section Abbreviation - Glove Serial Number). Section Abbreviation: Unique alphanumeric code assigned per section. Glove Serial Number: Numeric sequence (001-999). Marking: The control number is clearly marked (approximately 10mm square, black permanent marker) on the back of the wrist of each glove.  4. Inspection Pass Indication: Each glove is stamped with the date of passing the inspection. This date determines the next inspection date.   5. Manufacturer's Inspection Report:   Content: Includes external inspection results and pressure resistance test results (both must be "good"). Additionally, it must legally include the manufacturer's name, inspection date, method, location, inspector's name, and any remedial actions taken. High Voltage Test Values: 20,000V (new), 10,000V (periodic). Low Voltage Test Values: 3,000V (new), 1,500V (periodic). Defective Gloves: Gloves marked as "defective" must be discarded and replaced with spares. Inspection Provider: Kyowa PV Co., Ltd. (via WWW).  6. Record Retention: Inspection charts and reports must be retained as quality records for a minimum of three years, as required by law.

qf:English_FQ510-050_Handa_Tank_Cleaning.answer: Based on the provided pdf, the following safety equipment should be worn during solder bath maintenance:   Gloves: Double layered; inner cotton gloves [Made by ZZZ: Menu Stebukuro (No. 2001) equivalent] and outer heat-resistant (leather) gloves [Made by ZZZ: Leather Gloves (TTT 100 5) Equivalent].

Solder bath maintenance procedures, based on the provided text:    Safety Precautions and System Shutdown: Stop the solder jet using the specified switch settings (Main switch: ON, Preparation: OFF, Operation: OFF). Wear appropriate safety equipment. Stop work and contact a supervisor if any abnormalities occur.    Lower the Lifter: Lower the lifter using the lowering lever.    Remove the Solder Tank: Pull out the solder tank using the drawer lever.    Nozzle Removal and Cleaning: Remove nozzle fixing screws. Carefully remove nozzles using pliers, collecting spilled solder. Disassemble and clean nozzles, removing solder oxides from the straightening plate, wire mesh, and jet guide. (At least weekly). Consult the manufacturer's manual for proper disassembly procedures.    Nozzle Reassembly: Reassemble the nozzles, ensuring a secure fit to prevent floating or tilting.    Raise the Lifter: Raise the lifter using the lift lever.    Soldering Condition Verification: Check wave height, width, and solder level to ensure they meet specifications.    Resume Soldering Operation: Once soldering conditions are confirmed to be within the prescribed range and soldering performance is satisfactory, resume the soldering operation.

Post-task actions for solder bath inspection, based on the provided text, include:   Cleaning the flow soldering device: Wipe the upper part of the machine with a rag (replace regularly) or use an adhesive roll in cooler areas. Dispose of used adhesive rolls properly. Cleaning SUS plates: Remove dust and dross from stainless steel (SUS) plates. Cleaning safety shoes: Remove solder dust from dedicated safety shoes using an adhesive sheet. Personal hygiene: Wash hands with soap and gargle.
